[←20.17. Byte Arrays](Basic-Types/Byte-Arrays/#ByteArray "20.17. Byte Arrays")[20.19. Maps and Sets→](Basic-Types/Maps-and-Sets/#maps "20.19. Maps and Sets")
#  20.18. Ranges[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.section&name=ranges "Permalink")
A _range_ represents a series of consecutive elements of some type, from a lower bound to an upper bound. The bounds may be open, in which case the bound value is not part of the range, or closed, in which case the bound value is part of the range. Either bound may be omitted, in which case the range extends infinitely in the corresponding direction.
Ranges have dedicated syntax that consists of a starting point, `...`, and an ending point. The starting point may be either `*`, which denotes a range that continues infinitely downwards, or a term, which denotes a range with a specific starting value. By default, ranges are left-closed: they contain their starting points. A trailing `<` indicates that the range is left-open and does not contain its starting point. The ending point may be `*`, in which case the range continues infinitely upwards, or a term, which denotes a range with a specific ending value. By default, ranges are right-open: they do not contain their ending points. The ending point may be prefixed with `<` to indicate that it is right-open; this is the default and does not change the meaning, but may be easier to read. It may also be prefixed with `=` to indicate that the range is right-closed and contains its ending point.
Ranges of Natural Numbers
The range that contains the numbers `3` through `6` can be written in a variety of ways:
``[[](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")3[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 4[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 5[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 6[]](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") (3...7).[toList](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___toList "Documentation for Std.Rco.toList") `
```
[[](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")3[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 4[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 5[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 6[]](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")
```
``[[](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")3[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 4[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 5[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 6[]](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") (3...=6).[toList](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rcc___toList "Documentation for Std.Rcc.toList") `
```
[[](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")3[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 4[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 5[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 6[]](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")
```
``[[](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")3[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 4[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 5[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 6[]](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") (2<...=6).[toList](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Roc___toList "Documentation for Std.Roc.toList") `
```
[[](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")3[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 4[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 5[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") 6[]](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")

Finite and Infinite Ranges
This range cannot be converted to a list, because it is infinite:
`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") `failed to synthesize instance of type class   [Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rxi___IsAlwaysFinite___mk "Documentation for Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite") [Nat](Basic-Types/Natural-Numbers/#Nat___zero "Documentation for Nat")  Hint: Type class instance resolution failures can be inspected with the `set_option trace.Meta.synthInstance true` command.`(3...*).[toList](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rci___toList "Documentation for Std.Rci.toList") `
Finiteness of a left-closed, right-unbounded range is indicated by the presence of an instance of `[Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rxi___IsAlwaysFinite___mk "Documentation for Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite")`, which does not exist for `[Nat](Basic-Types/Natural-Numbers/#Nat___zero "Documentation for Nat")`. `[Std.Rco](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco")` is the type of these ranges, and the name `[Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rxi___IsAlwaysFinite___mk "Documentation for Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite")` indicates that it determines finiteness for all right-unbounded ranges.

```
failed to synthesize instance of type class
  [Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rxi___IsAlwaysFinite___mk "Documentation for Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite") [Nat](Basic-Types/Natural-Numbers/#Nat___zero "Documentation for Nat")

Hint: Type class instance resolution failures can be inspected with the `set_option trace.Meta.synthInstance true` command.
```

Attempting to enumerate the negative integers leads to a similar error, this time because there is no way to determine the least element:
`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") `failed to synthesize instance of type class   [Std.PRange.Least?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___Least______mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.Least?") [Int](Basic-Types/Integers/#Int___ofNat "Documentation for Int")  Hint: Type class instance resolution failures can be inspected with the `set_option trace.Meta.synthInstance true` command.`(*...(0 : [Int](Basic-Types/Integers/#Int___ofNat "Documentation for Int"))).[toList](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rio___toList "Documentation for Std.Rio.toList") `
```
failed to synthesize instance of type class
  [Std.PRange.Least?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___Least______mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.Least?") [Int](Basic-Types/Integers/#Int___ofNat "Documentation for Int")

Hint: Type class instance resolution failures can be inspected with the `set_option trace.Meta.synthInstance true` command.
```

Unbounded ranges in finite types indicate that the range extends to the greatest element of the type. Because `[UInt8](Basic-Types/Fixed-Precision-Integers/#UInt8___ofBitVec "Documentation for UInt8")` has 256 elements, this range contains 253 elements:
``253`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") ((3 : [UInt8](Basic-Types/Fixed-Precision-Integers/#UInt8___ofBitVec "Documentation for UInt8"))...*).[toArray](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rci___toArray "Documentation for Std.Rci.toArray").[size](Basic-Types/Arrays/#Array___size "Documentation for Array.size") `
```
253
```

[Live ↪](javascript:openLiveLink\("MQUwbghgNgBAFHAzDAXDAqgSQHYBcAcAlAHSkBUJuA9gIIBOdEAnsQM4CWAXiEA"\))
syntaxRange Syntax
This range is left-closed, right-open, and indicates `[Std.Rco](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


a...b is the range of elements greater than or equal to a and less than b.
See also Std.Rco.


term...term
```

This range is left-closed, right-open, and indicates `[Std.Rco](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


a...<b is the range of elements greater than or equal to a and less than b.
See also Std.Rco.


term...<term
```

This range is left-closed, right-closed, and indicates `[Std.Rcc](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rcc___mk "Documentation for Std.Rcc")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


a...=b is the range of elements greater than or equal to a and less than or equal to
b. See also Std.Rcc.


term...=term
```

This range is left-closed, right-infinite, and indicates `[Std.Rci](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rci___mk "Documentation for Std.Rci")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


a...* is the range of elements greater than or equal to a. See also Std.Rci. 


term...*
```

This range is left-open, right-open, and indicates `[Std.Roo](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Roo___mk "Documentation for Std.Roo")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


a<...b is the range of elements greater than a and less than b.
See also Std.Roo.


term<...term
```

This range is left-open, right-open, and indicates `[Std.Roo](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Roo___mk "Documentation for Std.Roo")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


a<...<b is the range of elements greater than a and less than b.
See also Std.Roo.


term<...<term
```

This range is left-open, right-closed, and indicates `[Std.Roc](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Roc___mk "Documentation for Std.Roc")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


a<...=b is the range of elements greater than a and less than or equal to b.
See also Std.Roc.


term<...=term
```

This range is left-open, right-infinite, and indicates `[Std.Roi](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Roi___mk "Documentation for Std.Roi")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


a<...* is the range of elements greater than a. See also Std.Roi. 


term<...*
```

This range is left-infinite, right-open, and indicates `[Std.Rio](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rio___mk "Documentation for Std.Rio")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


*...b is the range of elements less than b. See also Std.Rio. 


*...term
```

This range is left-infinite, right-open, and indicates `[Std.Ric](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Ric___mk "Documentation for Std.Ric")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


*...<b is the range of elements less than b. See also Std.Rio. 


*...<term
```

This range is left-infinite, right-closed, and indicates `[Std.Ric](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Ric___mk "Documentation for Std.Ric")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


*...=b is the range of elements less than or equal to b. See also Std.Ric. 


*...=term
```

This range is infinite on both sides, and indicates `[Std.Rii](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rii___mk "Documentation for Std.Rii")`:

```
term ::= ...
    | 


*...* is the range that is unbounded in both directions. See also Std.Rii. 


*...*
```

##  20.18.1. Range Types[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.section&name=The-Lean-Language-Reference--Basic-Types--Ranges--Range-Types "Permalink")
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rco "Permalink")structure
```


Std.Rco.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


Std.Rco.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


```

A range of elements of `α` with a closed lower bound and an open upper bound.
`a...b` or `a...<b` is the range of all values greater than or equal to `a : α` and less than `b : α`. This is notation for `Rco.mk a b`.
#  Constructor

```
[Std.Rco.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco.mk").{u}
```

#  Fields

```
lower : α
```

The lower bound of the range. `lower` is included in the range.

```
upper : α
```

The upper bound of the range. `upper` is not included in the range.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rco.iter "Permalink")def
```


Std.Rco.iter.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} (r : [Std.Rco](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco") α) : [Std.Iter](Iterators/Iterator-Definitions/#Std___Iter___mk "Documentation for Std.Iter") α


Std.Rco.iter.{u_1} {α : Type u_1}
  (r : [Std.Rco](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco") α) : [Std.Iter](Iterators/Iterator-Definitions/#Std___Iter___mk "Documentation for Std.Iter") α


```

Returns an iterator over the given range. This iterator will emit the elements of the range in increasing order.

Checks whether the range contains any value.
This function returns a meaningful value given `LawfulUpwardEnumerable` and `LawfulUpwardEnumerableLT` instances.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rcc.lower "Permalink")structure
```


Std.Rcc.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


Std.Rcc.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


```

A range of elements of `α` with closed lower and upper bounds.
`a...=b` is the range of all values greater than or equal to `a : α` and less than or equal to `b : α`. This is notation for `Rcc.mk a b`.
#  Constructor

```
[Std.Rcc.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rcc___mk "Documentation for Std.Rcc.mk").{u}
```

#  Fields

```
lower : α
```

The lower bound of the range. `lower` is included in the range.

```
upper : α
```

The upper bound of the range. `upper` is included in the range.

Checks whether the range contains any value.
This function returns a meaningful value given `LawfulUpwardEnumerable` and `LawfulUpwardEnumerableLE` instances.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rci.lower "Permalink")structure
```


Std.Rci.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


Std.Rci.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


```

An upward-unbounded range of elements of `α` with a closed lower bound.
`a...*` is the range of all values greater than or equal to `a : α`. This is notation for `Rci.mk a`.
#  Constructor

```
[Std.Rci.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rci___mk "Documentation for Std.Rci.mk").{u}
```

#  Fields

```
lower : α
```

The lower bound of the range. `lower` is included in the range.

```

Returns an iterator over the given range. This iterator will emit the elements of the range in increasing order.

Checks whether the range contains any value. This function exists for completeness and always returns false: The closed lower bound is contained in the range, so left-closed right-unbounded ranges are never empty.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Roo.lower "Permalink")structure
```


Std.Roo.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


Std.Roo.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


```

A range of elements of `α` with an open lower and upper bounds.
`a<...b` or `a<...<b` is the range of all values greater than `a : α` and less than `b : α`. This is notation for `Roo.mk a b`.
#  Constructor

```
[Std.Roo.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Roo___mk "Documentation for Std.Roo.mk").{u}
```

#  Fields

```
lower : α
```

The lower bound of the range. `lower` is not included in the range.

```
upper : α
```

The upper bound of the range. `upper` is not included in the range.

```

```

Returns an iterator over the given range. This iterator will emit the elements of the range in increasing order.

Std.Roc.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u

A range of elements of `α` with an open lower bound and a closed upper bound.
`a<...=b` is the range of all values greater than `a : α` and less than or equal to `b : α`. This is notation for `Roc.mk a b`.

```
upper : α
```

The upper bound of the range. `upper` is included in the range.

Checks whether the range contains any value.
This function returns a meaningful value given a `LawfulUpwardEnumerable` instance.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rio "Permalink")structure
```


Std.Rio.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


Std.Rio.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


```

A downward-unbounded range of elements of `α` with an open upper bound.
`*...b` or `*...<b` is the range of all values less than `b : α`. This is notation for `Rio.mk b`.
#  Constructor

```
[Std.Rio.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rio___mk "Documentation for Std.Rio.mk").{u}
```

#  Fields

```
upper : α
```

The upper bound of the range. `upper` is not included in the range.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rio.iter "Permalink")def
```


Std.Rio.iter.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} [[Std.PRange.Least?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___Least______mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.Least?") α]
  (r : [Std.Rio](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rio___mk "Documentation for Std.Rio") α) : [Std.Iter](Iterators/Iterator-Definitions/#Std___Iter___mk "Documentation for Std.Iter") α

  [Std.Iter](Iterators/Iterator-Definitions/#Std___Iter___mk "Documentation for Std.Iter") α


```

Returns an iterator over the given range. This iterator will emit the elements of the range in increasing order.

Checks whether the range contains any value.
This function returns a meaningful value given `LawfulUpwardEnumerable`, `LawfulUpwardEnumerableLT` and `LawfulUpwardEnumerableLeast?` instances.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Ric.upper "Permalink")structure
```


Std.Ric.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


Std.Ric.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


```

A downward-unbounded range of elements of `α` with a closed upper bound.
`*...=b` is the range of all values less than or equal to `b : α`. This is notation for `Ric.mk b`.
#  Constructor

```
[Std.Ric.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Ric___mk "Documentation for Std.Ric.mk").{u}
```

#  Fields

```
upper : α
```

The upper bound of the range. `upper` is included in the range.

Checks whether the range contains any value. This function exists for completeness and always returns false: The closed upper bound is contained in the range, so left-unbounded right-closed ranges are never empty.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rii.mk "Permalink")structure
```


Std.Rii.{u} (α : Type u) : Type


Std.Rii.{u} (α : Type u) : Type


```

A full range of all elements of `α`. Its only inhabitant is the range `*...*`, which is notation for `Rii.mk`.

```
[Std.Rii.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rii___mk "Documentation for Std.Rii.mk").{u}

  [[Std.Iterators.Finite](Iterators/Iterator-Definitions/#Std___Iterators___Finite___mk "Documentation for Std.Iterators.Finite") (Std.Rxi.Iterator α) [Id](Functors___-Monads-and--do--Notation/Varieties-of-Monads/#Id "Documentation for Id")] : [List](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List") α


Std.Rii.toList.{u} {α : Type u}
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α]

```

Returns the elements of the given full range as a list in ascending order.

```


Std.Rii.size.{u} {α : Type u} :

Returns the number of elements contained in the full range.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rii.isEmpty "Permalink")def
```


Std.Rii.isEmpty.{u} {α : Type u} [[Std.PRange.Least?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___Least______mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.Least?") α] :

Checks whether the range contains any value.
This function returns a meaningful value given `LawfulUpwardEnumerable` and `LawfulUpwardEnumerableLeast?` instances.
##  20.18.2. Range-Related Type Classes[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.section&name=The-Lean-Language-Reference--Basic-Types--Ranges--Range-Related-Type-Classes "Permalink")
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable "Permalink")type class
```


Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.{u}
  (α : Type u) : Type u


```

This typeclass provides the function `succ? : α → [Option](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option") α` that computes the successor of elements of `α`, or none if no successor exists. It also provides the function `succMany?`, which computes `n`-th successors.
`succ?` is expected to be acyclic: No element is its own transitive successor. If `α` is ordered, then every element larger than `a : α` should be a transitive successor of `a`. These properties and the compatibility of `succ?` with `succMany?` are encoded in the typeclasses `LawfulUpwardEnumerable`, `LawfulUpwardEnumerableLE` and `LawfulUpwardEnumerableLT`.
#  Instance Constructor

```
[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.mk").{u}
```

#  Methods

```
succ? : α → [Option](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option") α
```

Maps elements of `α` to their successor, or none if no successor exists.

```
succMany? : [Nat](Basic-Types/Natural-Numbers/#Nat___zero "Documentation for Nat") → α → [Option](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option") α
```

Maps elements of `α` to their `n`-th successor, or none if no successor exists. This should semantically behave like repeatedly applying `succ?`, but it might be more efficient.
`LawfulUpwardEnumerable` ensures the compatibility with `succ?`.
If no other implementation is provided in `UpwardEnumerable` instance, `succMany?` repeatedly applies `succ?`.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.LE "Permalink")def
```


Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.LE.{u} {α : Type u}
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] (a b : α) : Prop


Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.LE.{u}
  {α : Type u}
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α]
  (a b : α) : Prop


```

According to `UpwardEnumerable.LE`, `a` is less than or equal to `b` if `b` is `a` or a transitive successor of `a`.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.LT "Permalink")def
```


Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.LT.{u} {α : Type u}
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] (a b : α) : Prop


Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.LT.{u}
  {α : Type u}
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α]
  (a b : α) : Prop


```

According to `UpwardEnumerable.LT`, `a` is less than `b` if `b` is a proper transitive successor of `a`. 'Proper' means that `b` is the `n`-th successor of `a`, where `n > 0`.
Given `LawfulUpwardEnumerable α`, no element of `α` is less than itself.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerable.mk "Permalink")type class
```


Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerable.{u} (α : Type u)
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] : Prop


Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerable.{u}
  (α : Type u)
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] : Prop


```

This typeclass ensures that an `UpwardEnumerable α` instance is well-behaved.
#  Instance Constructor

```
[Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerable.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___LawfulUpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerable.mk").{u}
```

#  Methods

```
ne_of_lt : ∀ (a b : α), [Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.LT](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___LT "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.LT") a b → a ≠ b
```

There is no cyclic chain of successors.

```
succMany?_zero : ∀ (a : α), [Std.PRange.succMany?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?") 0 a [=](Basic-Propositions/Propositional-Equality/#Eq___refl "Documentation for Eq") [some](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.some") a
```

The `0`-th successor of `a` is `a` itself.

```
succMany?_add_one : ∀ (n : [Nat](Basic-Types/Natural-Numbers/#Nat___zero "Documentation for Nat")) (a : α), [Std.PRange.succMany?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?") [(](Type-Classes/Basic-Classes/#HAdd___mk "Documentation for HAdd.hAdd")n [+](Type-Classes/Basic-Classes/#HAdd___mk "Documentation for HAdd.hAdd") 1[)](Type-Classes/Basic-Classes/#HAdd___mk "Documentation for HAdd.hAdd") a [=](Basic-Propositions/Propositional-Equality/#Eq___refl "Documentation for Eq") ([Std.PRange.succMany?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?") n a).[bind](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___bind "Documentation for Option.bind") [Std.PRange.succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?")
```

The `n + 1`-th successor of `a` is the successor of the `n`-th successor, given that said successors actually exist.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.PRange.Least?.mk "Permalink")type class
```


Std.PRange.Least?.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


Std.PRange.Least?.{u} (α : Type u) :
  Type u


```

The typeclass `Least? α` optionally provides a smallest element of `α`, `least? : [Option](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option") α`.
The main use case of this typeclass is to use it in combination with `UpwardEnumerable` to obtain a (possibly infinite) ascending enumeration of all elements of `α`.
#  Instance Constructor

```
[Std.PRange.Least?.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___Least______mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.Least?.mk").{u}
```

#  Methods

```
least? : [Option](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option") α
```

Returns the smallest element of `α`, or none if `α` is empty.
Only empty types are allowed to define `least? := none`. If `α` is ordered and nonempty, then the value of `least?` should be the smallest element according to the order on `α`.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.PRange.InfinitelyUpwardEnumerable.mk "Permalink")type class
```


Std.PRange.InfinitelyUpwardEnumerable.{u} (α : Type u)
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] : Prop


Std.PRange.InfinitelyUpwardEnumerable.{u}
  (α : Type u)
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] : Prop


```

This propositional typeclass ensures that `UpwardEnumerable.succ?` will never return `[none](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.none")`. In other words, it ensures that there will always be a successor.
#  Instance Constructor

```
[Std.PRange.InfinitelyUpwardEnumerable.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___InfinitelyUpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.InfinitelyUpwardEnumerable.mk").{u}
```

#  Methods

```
isSome_succ? : ∀ (a : α), ([Std.PRange.succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?") a).[isSome](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___isSome "Documentation for Option.isSome") [=](Basic-Propositions/Propositional-Equality/#Eq___refl "Documentation for Eq") [true](Basic-Types/Booleans/#Bool___false "Documentation for Bool.true")
```

Every element of `α` has a successor.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.PRange.LinearlyUpwardEnumerable.eq_of_succ?_eq "Permalink")type class
```


Std.PRange.LinearlyUpwardEnumerable.{u} (α : Type u)
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] : Prop


Std.PRange.LinearlyUpwardEnumerable.{u}
  (α : Type u)
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] : Prop


```

This propositional typeclass ensures that `UpwardEnumerable.succ?` is injective.
#  Instance Constructor

```
[Std.PRange.LinearlyUpwardEnumerable.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___LinearlyUpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.LinearlyUpwardEnumerable.mk").{u}
```

#  Methods

```
eq_of_succ?_eq : ∀ (a b : α), [Std.PRange.succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?") a [=](Basic-Propositions/Propositional-Equality/#Eq___refl "Documentation for Eq") [Std.PRange.succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?") b → a [=](Basic-Propositions/Propositional-Equality/#Eq___refl "Documentation for Eq") b
```

The implementation of `UpwardEnumerable.succ?` for `α` is injective.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite "Permalink")type class
```


Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite.{u} (α : Type u)
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] : Prop


Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite.{u} (α : Type u)
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] : Prop


```

This type class ensures that right-unbounded ranges (i.e., for a bound `a`, `a...*`, `a<...*` and `*...*`) are always finite. This is a prerequisite for many functions and instances, such as `Rci.toList` or `[ForIn'](Functors___-Monads-and--do--Notation/Syntax/#ForIn______mk "Documentation for ForIn'")`.
#  Instance Constructor

```
[Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rxi___IsAlwaysFinite___mk "Documentation for Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite.mk").{u}
```

#  Methods

```
finite : ∀ (init : α), [∃](Basic-Propositions/Quantifiers/#Exists___intro "Documentation for Exists") n[,](Basic-Propositions/Quantifiers/#Exists___intro "Documentation for Exists") [Std.PRange.succMany?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?") n init [=](Basic-Propositions/Propositional-Equality/#Eq___refl "Documentation for Eq") [none](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.none")
```

For every elements `init`, there exists a chain of successors that results in an element that has no successors.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rxi.HasSize "Permalink")type class
```


Std.Rxi.HasSize.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


Std.Rxi.HasSize.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


```

This typeclass provides support for the size function for ranges with closed lower bound (`Ric.size`, `Rio.size` and `Rii.size`).
The returned size should be equal to the number of elements returned by `toList`. This condition is captured by the typeclass `LawfulHasSize`.
#  Instance Constructor

```
[Std.Rxi.HasSize.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rxi___HasSize___mk "Documentation for Std.Rxi.HasSize.mk").{u}
```

#  Methods

```
size : α → [Nat](Basic-Types/Natural-Numbers/#Nat___zero "Documentation for Nat")
```

Returns the number of elements starting from `lo` that satisfy the given upper bound.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rxc.IsAlwaysFinite.mk "Permalink")type class
```


Std.Rxc.IsAlwaysFinite.{u} (α : Type u) [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α]
  [[LE](Type-Classes/Basic-Classes/#LE___mk "Documentation for LE") α] : Prop


Std.Rxc.IsAlwaysFinite.{u} (α : Type u)
  [[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") α] [[LE](Type-Classes/Basic-Classes/#LE___mk "Documentation for LE") α] :
  Prop


```

This type class ensures that right-closed ranges (i.e., for bounds `a` and `b`, `a...=b`, `a<...=b` and `*...=b`) are always finite. This is a prerequisite for many functions and instances, such as `Rcc.toList` or `[ForIn'](Functors___-Monads-and--do--Notation/Syntax/#ForIn______mk "Documentation for ForIn'")`.
#  Instance Constructor

```
[Std.Rxc.IsAlwaysFinite.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rxc___IsAlwaysFinite___mk "Documentation for Std.Rxc.IsAlwaysFinite.mk").{u}
```

#  Methods

```
finite : ∀ (init hi : α), [∃](Basic-Propositions/Quantifiers/#Exists___intro "Documentation for Exists") n[,](Basic-Propositions/Quantifiers/#Exists___intro "Documentation for Exists") ([Std.PRange.succMany?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?") n init).[elim](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___elim "Documentation for Option.elim") [True](Basic-Propositions/Truth/#True___intro "Documentation for True") fun x => [¬](Basic-Propositions/Logical-Connectives/#Not "Documentation for Not")x [≤](Type-Classes/Basic-Classes/#LE___mk "Documentation for LE.le") hi
```

For every pair of elements `init` and `hi`, there exists a chain of successors that results in an element that either has no successors or is greater than `hi`.
[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rxc.HasSize.size "Permalink")type class
```


Std.Rxc.HasSize.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


Std.Rxc.HasSize.{u} (α : Type u) : Type u


```

This typeclass provides support for the size function for ranges with closed lower bound (`Rcc.size`, `Rco.size` and `Rci.size`).
The returned size should be equal to the number of elements returned by `toList`. This condition is captured by the typeclass `LawfulHasSize`.
#  Instance Constructor

```
[Std.Rxc.HasSize.mk](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rxc___HasSize___mk "Documentation for Std.Rxc.HasSize.mk").{u}
```

#  Methods

```
size : α → α → [Nat](Basic-Types/Natural-Numbers/#Nat___zero "Documentation for Nat")
```

Returns the number of elements starting from `lo` that satisfy the given upper bound.
##  20.18.3. Implementing Ranges[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.section&name=The-Lean-Language-Reference--Basic-Types--Ranges--Implementing-Ranges "Permalink")
The built-in range types may be used with any type, but their usefulness depends on the presence of certain type class instances. Generally speaking, ranges are either checked for membership, enumerated or iterated over. To check whether an value is contained in a range, `[DecidableLT](Type-Classes/Basic-Classes/#DecidableLT "Documentation for DecidableLT")` and `[DecidableLE](Type-Classes/Basic-Classes/#DecidableLE "Documentation for DecidableLE")` instances are used to compare the value to the range's respective open and closed endpoints. To get an iterator for a range, instances of `[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable")` and `[Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___LawfulUpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerable")` are all that's needed. To iterate directly over it in a ``Lean.Parser.Term.doFor : doElem`
`for x in e do s` iterates over `e` assuming `e`'s type has an instance of the `ForIn` typeclass. `break` and `continue` are supported inside `for` loops. `for x in e, x2 in e2, ... do s` iterates of the given collections in parallel, until at least one of them is exhausted. The types of `e2` etc. must implement the `Std.ToStream` typeclass.
``for` loop, `Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerableLE` and `Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerableLT` are required as well. To enumerate a range (e.g. by calling `[toList](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___toList "Documentation for Std.Rco.toList")`), it must be proven finite. This is done by supplying instances of `[Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rxi___IsAlwaysFinite___mk "Documentation for Std.Rxi.IsAlwaysFinite")`, `[Std.Rxc.IsAlwaysFinite](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rxc___IsAlwaysFinite___mk "Documentation for Std.Rxc.IsAlwaysFinite")`, or `Std.Rxo.IsAlwaysFinite`.
Implementing Ranges
The enumeration type `[Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")` represents the days of the week:
`inductive Day where   | mo | tu | we | th | fr | sa | su deriving [Repr](Interacting-with-Lean/#Repr___mk "Documentation for Repr") `
While it's already possible to use this type in ranges, they're not particularly useful. There's no membership instance:
`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") `failed to synthesize instance of type class   Membership [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") ([Std.Rcc](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rcc___mk "Documentation for Std.Rcc") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example"))  Hint: Type class instance resolution failures can be inspected with the `set_option trace.Meta.synthInstance true` command.`[Day.we](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___we-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") ∈ ([Day.mo](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___mo-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")...=[Day.fr](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___fr-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")) `
```
failed to synthesize instance of type class
  Membership [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") ([Std.Rcc](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rcc___mk "Documentation for Std.Rcc") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example"))

Hint: Type class instance resolution failures can be inspected with the `set_option trace.Meta.synthInstance true` command.
```

Ranges can't be iterated over:
`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") show [IO](IO/Logical-Model/#IO "Documentation for IO") [Unit](Basic-Types/The-Unit-Type/#Unit "Documentation for Unit") from   `failed to synthesize instance for 'for_in%' notation   [ForIn](Functors___-Monads-and--do--Notation/Syntax/#ForIn___mk "Documentation for ForIn") ([EIO](IO/Logical-Model/#EIO "Documentation for EIO") [IO.Error](IO/Logical-Model/#IO___Error___alreadyExists "Documentation for IO.Error")) ([Std.Rcc](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rcc___mk "Documentation for Std.Rcc") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")) ?m.11`for d in [Day.mo](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___mo-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")...=[Day.fr](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___fr-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") do IO.println s!"It's {repr d}" `
```
failed to synthesize instance for 'for_in%' notation
  [ForIn](Functors___-Monads-and--do--Notation/Syntax/#ForIn___mk "Documentation for ForIn") ([EIO](IO/Logical-Model/#EIO "Documentation for EIO") [IO.Error](IO/Logical-Model/#IO___Error___alreadyExists "Documentation for IO.Error")) ([Std.Rcc](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rcc___mk "Documentation for Std.Rcc") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")) ?m.11
```

Nor can they be enumerated, even though the type is finite:
`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") `failed to synthesize instance of type class   [Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")  Hint: Type class instance resolution failures can be inspected with the `set_option trace.Meta.synthInstance true` command.`([Day.sa](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___sa-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")...*).[toList](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rci___toList "Documentation for Std.Rci.toList") `
```

Membership tests require `[DecidableLT](Type-Classes/Basic-Classes/#DecidableLT "Documentation for DecidableLT")` and `[DecidableLE](Type-Classes/Basic-Classes/#DecidableLE "Documentation for DecidableLE")` instances. An easy way to get these is to number each day, and compare the numbers:

With these instances available, membership tests work as expected:
`def Day.isWeekday (d : [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")) : [Bool](Basic-Types/Booleans/#Bool___false "Documentation for Bool") := d ∈ [Day.mo](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___mo-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")...[Day.sa](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___sa-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") ```[true](Basic-Types/Booleans/#Bool___false "Documentation for Bool.true")`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") [Day.th](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___th-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example").[isWeekday](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___isWeekday-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") `

Iteration and enumeration are both variants on repeatedly applying a successor function until either the upper bound of the range or the largest element of the type is reached. This successor function is `[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?")`. It's also convenient to have a definition of the function in `[Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")`'s namespace for use with generalized field notation:
`def Day.succ? : [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") → [Option](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")   | [mo](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___mo-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") => [some](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.some") [tu](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___tu-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")   | [tu](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___tu-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") => [some](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.some") [we](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___we-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")   | [we](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___we-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") => [some](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.some") [th](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___th-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")   | [th](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___th-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") => [some](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.some") [fr](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___fr-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")   | [fr](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___fr-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") => [some](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.some") [sa](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___sa-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")   | [sa](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___sa-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") => [some](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.some") [su](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___su-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")   | [su](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___su-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") => [none](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.none")  instance : [Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") where   [succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?") := [Day.succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___succ___-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") `
Iteration also requires a proof that the implementation of `[succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?")` is sensible. Its properties are expressed in terms of `[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?")`, which iterates the application of `[succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?")` a certain number of times and has a default implementation in terms of `[Nat.repeat](Basic-Types/Natural-Numbers/#Nat___repeat "Documentation for Nat.repeat")` and `[succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?")`. In particular, an instance of `[LawfulUpwardEnumerable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___LawfulUpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerable")` requires proofs that `[Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?")` corresponds to the default implementation along with a proof that repeatedly applying the successor never yields the same element again.
The first step is to write two helper lemmas for the two proofs about `[succMany?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?")`. While they could be written inline in the instance declaration, it's convenient for them to have the `[@[](Attributes/#Lean___Parser___Term___attributes-next "Documentation for syntax")simp[]](Attributes/#Lean___Parser___Term___attributes-next "Documentation for syntax")` attribute.

(n + 1) d =     ([Std.PRange.succMany?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?") n d).[bind](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___bind "Documentation for Option.bind") [Std.PRange.succ?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succ?") := byn:[Nat](Basic-Types/Natural-Numbers/#Nat___zero "Documentation for Nat")d:[Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")⊢ [Std.PRange.succMany?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___UpwardEnumerable___mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.UpwardEnumerable.succMany?") Proving that there are no cycles in successor uses a convenient helper lemma that calculates the number of successor steps between any two days. It is marked `[@[](Attributes/#Lean___Parser___Term___attributes-next "Documentation for syntax")grind →[]](Attributes/#Lean___Parser___Term___attributes-next "Documentation for syntax")` because when assumptions that match its premises are present, it adds a great deal of new information:

[Day.mo, Day.tu, Day.we, Day.th, Day.fr]
```

Adding a `[Std.PRange.Least?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___Least______mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.Least?")` instance allows enumeration of left-unbounded ranges:
`instance : [Std.PRange.Least?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___Least______mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.Least?") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") where   [least?](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___PRange___Least______mk "Documentation for Std.PRange.Least?.least?") := [some](Basic-Types/Optional-Values/#Option___none "Documentation for Option.some") [.mo](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___mo-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")  def allWeekdays' : [List](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") := (*...[Day.sa](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___sa-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")).[toList](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rio___toList "Documentation for Std.Rio.toList") `[Day.mo, Day.tu, Day.we, Day.th, Day.fr]`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") [allWeekdays'](Basic-Types/Ranges/#allWeekdays___-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") `
```
[Day.mo, Day.tu, Day.we, Day.th, Day.fr]
```

It's also possible to create an iterator that can be enumerated, but it can't yet be used with ``Lean.Parser.Term.doFor : doElem`
`for x in e do s` iterates over `e` assuming `e`'s type has an instance of the `ForIn` typeclass. `break` and `continue` are supported inside `for` loops. `for x in e, x2 in e2, ... do s` iterates of the given collections in parallel, until at least one of them is exhausted. The types of `e2` etc. must implement the `Std.ToStream` typeclass.
``for`:
``[Day.we, Day.th]`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") ([Day.we](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___we-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")...[Day.fr](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___fr-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")).[iter](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___iter "Documentation for Std.Rco.iter").[toList](Iterators/Consuming-Iterators/#Std___Iter___toList "Documentation for Std.Iter.toList") `
```
[Day.we, Day.th]
```
`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") show [IO](IO/Logical-Model/#IO "Documentation for IO") [Unit](Basic-Types/The-Unit-Type/#Unit "Documentation for Unit") from [do](Functors___-Monads-and--do--Notation/Syntax/#Lean___Parser___Term___do "Documentation for syntax")   `failed to synthesize instance for 'for_in%' notation   [ForIn](Functors___-Monads-and--do--Notation/Syntax/#ForIn___mk "Documentation for ForIn") ([EIO](IO/Logical-Model/#EIO "Documentation for EIO") [IO.Error](IO/Logical-Model/#IO___Error___alreadyExists "Documentation for IO.Error")) ([Std.Iter](Iterators/Iterator-Definitions/#Std___Iter___mk "Documentation for Std.Iter") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")) ?m.12`for d in ([Day.mo](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___mo-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")...[Day.th](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___th-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")).[iter](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___iter "Documentation for Std.Rco.iter") do IO.println s!"It's {repr d}." `
```
failed to synthesize instance for 'for_in%' notation
  [ForIn](Functors___-Monads-and--do--Notation/Syntax/#ForIn___mk "Documentation for ForIn") ([EIO](IO/Logical-Model/#EIO "Documentation for EIO") [IO.Error](IO/Logical-Model/#IO___Error___alreadyExists "Documentation for IO.Error")) ([Std.Iter](Iterators/Iterator-Definitions/#Std___Iter___mk "Documentation for Std.Iter") [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")) ?m.12

The last step to enable iteration, thus making ranges of days fully-featured, is to prove that the less-than and less-than-or-equal-to relations on `[Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")` correspond to the notions of inequality that are derived from iterating the successor function. This is captured in the classes `Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerableLT` and `Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerableLE`, which require that the two notions are logically equivalent:
`instance : Std.PRange.LawfulUpwardEnumerableLT [Day](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example") where   lt_iff d1 d2 := [grind](Tactic-Proofs/Tactic-Reference/#grind "Documentation for tactic")All goals completed! 🐙 `
It is now possible to iterate over ranges of days:
``It's Day.mo It's Day.tu It's Day.we `[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") show [IO](IO/Logical-Model/#IO "Documentation for IO") [Unit](Basic-Types/The-Unit-Type/#Unit "Documentation for Unit") from [do](Functors___-Monads-and--do--Notation/Syntax/#Lean___Parser___Term___do "Documentation for syntax") for x in ([Day.mo](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___mo-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")...[Day.th](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Day___th-_LPAR_in-Implementing-Ranges_RPAR_ "Definition of example")).[iter](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___iter "Documentation for Std.Rco.iter") do [IO.println](IO/Console-Output/#IO___println "Documentation for IO.println") s!"It's {[repr](Interacting-with-Lean/#repr-next "Documentation for repr") x}" `
```
It's Day.mo
It's Day.tu
It's Day.we

```

##  20.18.4. Ranges and Slices[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.section&name=The-Lean-Language-Reference--Basic-Types--Ranges--Ranges-and-Slices "Permalink")
Range syntax can be used with data structures that support slicing to select a slice of the structure.
Slicing Lists
Lists may be sliced with any of the interval types:
`def groceries :=   ["apples", "bananas", "coffee", "dates", "endive", "fennel"] ```[[](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")"bananas"[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") "coffee"[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") "dates"[]](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") [groceries](Basic-Types/Ranges/#groceries-_LPAR_in-Slicing-Lists_RPAR_ "Definition of example")[1...4] |>.toList `
```
[[](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")"bananas"[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") "coffee"[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") "dates"[]](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")
```
``[[](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")"bananas"[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") "coffee"[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") "dates"[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") "endive"[]](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") [groceries](Basic-Types/Ranges/#groceries-_LPAR_in-Slicing-Lists_RPAR_ "Definition of example")[1...=4] |>.toList `
```
[[](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")"bananas"[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") "coffee"[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") "dates"[,](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons") "endive"[]](Basic-Types/Linked-Lists/#List___nil "Documentation for List.cons")
```

```

[Live ↪](javascript:openLiveLink\("CYUwZgBA5gTg9gYxDAliAzhAXAXgFAQQDaARAIYAOFANhiQDQQkBGZAdu2eg0wnGGBAgeJYGQAudRiRBtgKAG7DpgtmxDUSAXTx4AxCAVlq0eElQYiARgB0dgCxaIAHwB8N8XAAyKdON0GRiawiMho6NZ2NjiOLu6ePn4BhsamoRYRtnYAVE5uHt6+/vopwWZhllYAPFGx+QlFyUFp5uHWNXYxefGFSSXNIa2W2VFdcQWJxYGpgxURIw7dE439M+UZRAs2ueMNfkA"\))
Custom Slices
A `[Triple](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Triple-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example")` contains three values of the same type:
`structure Triple (α : Type u) where   fst : α   snd : α   thd : α deriving [Repr](Interacting-with-Lean/#Repr___mk "Documentation for Repr") `
Positions in a triple may be any of the fields, or just after `[thd](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Triple___thd-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example")`:
`inductive TriplePos where   | fst | snd | thd | done deriving [Repr](Interacting-with-Lean/#Repr___mk "Documentation for Repr") `
A slice of a triple consists of a triple, a starting position, and a stopping position. The starting position is inclusive, and the stopping position exclusive:
`structure TripleSlice (α : Type u) where   triple : [Triple](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Triple-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") α   start : [TriplePos](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TriplePos-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example")   stop : [TriplePos](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TriplePos-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") deriving [Repr](Interacting-with-Lean/#Repr___mk "Documentation for Repr") `
Ranges of `[TriplePos](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TriplePos-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example")` can be used to select a slice from a triple by implementing instances of each supported range type's `[Sliceable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___Sliceable___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco.Sliceable")` class. For example, `[Std.Rco.Sliceable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___Sliceable___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco.Sliceable")` allows left-closed, right-open ranges to be used to slice `[Triple](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Triple-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example")`s:
`instance : [Std.Rco.Sliceable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___Sliceable___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco.Sliceable") ([Triple](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Triple-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") α) [TriplePos](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TriplePos-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") ([TripleSlice](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TripleSlice-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") α) where   [mkSlice](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___Sliceable___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco.Sliceable.mkSlice") triple range :=     { [triple](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TripleSlice___triple-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example"), [start](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TripleSlice___start-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") := range.[lower](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco.lower"), [stop](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TripleSlice___stop-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") := range.[upper](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rco___mk "Documentation for Std.Rco.upper") } ``def abc : [Triple](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Triple-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") [Char](Basic-Types/Characters/#Char___mk "Documentation for Char") := ⟨'a', 'b', 'c'⟩  [open](Namespaces-and-Sections/#Lean___Parser___Command___open "Documentation for syntax") TriplePos [in](Namespaces-and-Sections/#Lean___Parser___Command___in "Documentation for syntax") `{ triple := { fst := 'a', snd := 'b', thd := 'c' }, start := TriplePos.snd, stop := TriplePos.thd }`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") [abc](Basic-Types/Ranges/#abc-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example")[[snd](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TriplePos___snd-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example")...[thd](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TriplePos___thd-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example")] `
```
{ triple := { fst := 'a', snd := 'b', thd := 'c' }, start := TriplePos.snd, stop := TriplePos.thd }
```

Infinite ranges have only a lower bound:
`instance : [Std.Rci.Sliceable](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rci___Sliceable___mk "Documentation for Std.Rci.Sliceable") ([Triple](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Triple-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") α) [TriplePos](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TriplePos-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") ([TripleSlice](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TripleSlice-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") α) where   [mkSlice](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rci___Sliceable___mk "Documentation for Std.Rci.Sliceable.mkSlice") triple range :=     { [triple](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TripleSlice___triple-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example"), [start](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TripleSlice___start-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") := range.[lower](Basic-Types/Ranges/#Std___Rci___mk "Documentation for Std.Rci.lower"), [stop](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TripleSlice___stop-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") := [.done](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TriplePos___done-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example") }  [open](Namespaces-and-Sections/#Lean___Parser___Command___open "Documentation for syntax") TriplePos [in](Namespaces-and-Sections/#Lean___Parser___Command___in "Documentation for syntax") `{ triple := { fst := 'a', snd := 'b', thd := 'c' }, start := TriplePos.snd, stop := TriplePos.done }`[#eval](Interacting-with-Lean/#Lean___Parser___Command___eval "Documentation for syntax") [abc](Basic-Types/Ranges/#abc-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example")[[snd](Basic-Types/Ranges/#TriplePos___snd-_LPAR_in-Custom-Slices_RPAR_ "Definition of example")...*] `
```
{ triple := { fst := 'a', snd := 'b', thd := 'c' }, start := TriplePos.snd, stop := TriplePos.done }
```

[🔗](find/?domain=Verso.Genre.Manual.doc&name=Std.Rco.Sliceable.mkSlice "Permalink")type class
```


Std.Rco.Sliceable.{u, v, w} (α : Type u) (β : [outParam](Type-Classes/Instance-Synthesis/#outParam "Documentation for outParam") (Type v))
  (γ : [outParam](Type-Classes/Instance-Synthesis/#outParam "Documentation for outParam") (Type w)) : Type (max (max u v) w)


Std.Rco.Sliceable.{u, v, w} (α : Type u)
  (β : [outParam](Type-Classes/Instance-Synthesis/#outParam "Documentation for outParam") (Type v))
  (γ : [outParam](Type-Classes/Instance-Synthesis/#outParam "Documentation for outParam") (Type w)) :
  Type (max (max u v) w)

The type of the resulting slices is `γ`.
